reviewer: agent matrix pass (headless Chrome, real download+run, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — real transformers.js@3.7.5 image-classification pipeline for Xenova/dit-base-finetuned-rvlcdip ran in a Web Worker (worker.js processor→model forward pass), WASM, q8 (model_quantized.onnx). Fresh-profile end-to-end: Download (~88 MB) → ready ~16 s → Classify returned real RVL-CDIP labels: default form sample → Form 78.9%, Presentation 5.3%, Scientific Report 4.0% (524 ms). Invoice sample → Invoice 37.6%, Budget 36.3%, Scientific Report 15.8% (16-class distribution). conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): calls-real-inference derived. Screenshots retained under /home/paulkinlan/tmp (shot-desktop-light.png, shot-inside-light.png, shot-runit-light.png).
rubric.notes: DISTINCT document-AI domain (16 RVL-CDIP types), not generic ImageNet ViT. Runnability pre-verified BEFORE build: standalone dynamic-import of the CDN build returned logitsShape [1,16], numClasses 16, real id2label (letter…memo), and classified a synthetic ruled-line page as 'form' 0.72 — confirms a REAL classification head, not a head-less feature export.

rubric.evidence: Output is a [1,16] logits tensor → softmax over exactly the 16 RVL-CDIP classes; pipe.model.config.id2label has 16 human labels (letter, form, email, handwritten, advertisement, scientific report, scientific publication, specification, file folder, news article, budget, invoice, presentation, questionnaire, resume, memo). Semantic sanity confirmed across generated samples: form→form 78.9%, memo→memo 86.8%, invoice→invoice 37.6%, email→email 45.5%, scientific paper→scientific report 68.4%.
rubric.notes: Because there are only 16 classes the 'See inside' surface renders the ENTIRE distribution (renderDistribution), plus top-1 margin and normalised Shannon entropy computed in-worker from the raw logits.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance table declares model id Xenova/dit-base-finetuned-rvlcdip (linked), task image-classification, architecture DiT/BEiT, quantisation q8, backend WebAssembly, ~88 MB, Apache-2.0 — matching what worker.js loads (loadPipeline task:image-classification, dtype:q8, backend:wasm) and what createModelLoader is told (modelId/runtime/dtype/sizeMB/requiresWebGPU:false). Readout shows the real backend used at runtime (WASM) and real latency.
rubric.notes: requiresWebGPU:false is honest — the BEiT/DiT q8 export runs on plain WASM; verified running with --disable-gpu headless.

rubric.evidence: Uses the shared lib/model-loader.js createModelLoader (no hand-rolled Load button). Fresh profile (cache-absent) → loader state 'download-required' with a 'Download model (~88 MB)' button (verified: initial state=download-required, button text captured). After download → state 'ready' and the Run button enables. A valid current cached copy auto-initialises with a checking→initialising→ready role=status line; a per-model 'Clear cached model' control + storage usage are provided by the shared loader.
rubric.notes: State machine (current/update/partial/absent) and integrity check are handled by the shared model-cache layer, consistent with every other page.

rubric.evidence: Loader status is role=status aria-live=polite and moves through Checking → Downloading N% → Ready; the per-run #runStatus is role=status aria-live=polite and reports Classifying… → Done / Failed. Errors surface ON THE PAGE via runStatus.classList.add('err') (never console-only), and the shared loader offers Retry on a failed init. Progress <progress> is shown during download only.
rubric.notes: Practical page reports live 'Sorting i/N…' progress; wild page shows 'Classifying composite…' with a debounced re-run; multimodel shows staged '1/2 — classifying…' / '2/2 — reading the page…'.

rubric.evidence: Every visible control exercised headless. Main: keyboard-operable dropzone (role=button, tabindex=0, Enter/Space) + hidden file input, sample thumbs (role=button + keydown), top-k range in a <label>, Classify button. Practical: multi-file dropzone, auto-file confidence-threshold slider, removable batch thumbs, Sort inbox. Wild: top/bottom type <select>s, seam slider, rotate slider (auto re-classify). Multimodel: free-text question input (auto-route when blank) + Classify & extract.
rubric.notes: Controls reuse the shared classify-ui.js CLASSIFY_CSS (chips/sliders/bars) so semantics match the rest of the showcase.

rubric.evidence: Every surface shows REAL model output. Bars/distribution are the actual softmax; the practical confidence gate uses the real top-1 probability (invoice 37.6% and email 45.5% correctly fell below the 60% threshold and routed to 'Needs review', while form/memo/scientific-report auto-filed); the wild composite genuinely flattens (letter+form splice → Form 42.3%, high entropy). Multimodel: Donut really read the synthetic invoice and answered '$588.60' (the rendered total) — a real second-model extraction, not a canned string.
rubric.notes: No canned results anywhere; failures degrade to an on-page error + Retry.

rubric.evidence: conformance images-have-alt / buttons-have-names / live-status-region derived from the markup: all sample/preview images carry alt text; all buttons have text names; loader + run status are aria-live regions; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow + a descriptive aria-label (shared renderBars). Custom dropzone/thumbs are keyboard-operable with roles. Light + dark both render with the shared WCAG-AA design tokens (see-inside panel legible in light mode screenshot).
rubric.notes: guidanceConsulted records the accessible-error-announcement + css-layout guidance applied.

rubric.evidence: Matrix verified at mobile 360×740 (DPR 3, touch) and desktop 1280×800. document.documentElement.scrollWidth ≤ innerWidth on ALL five pages (main, basics, practical, wild, multimodel) — no unintended horizontal overflow. Run panel is a flex .field-row with two flex:1 1 280px columns each min-inline-size:0; the 16-class dist-grid and the practical queues are repeat(auto-fit/auto-fill, minmax(...)) grids that stack cleanly on narrow screens; long labels use overflow-wrap:anywhere.
rubric.notes: Applied css-layout guidance (flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 + intrinsic grids). responsive-check --seed-support run before push.

rubric.evidence: All model load + inference run in a dedicated module Web Worker (worker.js) via the shared ClassifierEngine, so the main thread stays responsive during the ~524 ms forward pass — 0 console errors and the page remained interactive under headless eval during load. The wild page debounces re-classification (350 ms) and suppresses overlapping runs (single-flight with pendingAgain) so slider drags don't queue a backlog.
rubric.notes: Applied break-up-long-tasks guidance by keeping heavy work off the main thread; the small per-result DOM updates (≤16 bar/dist rows) are well under a frame.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel two-column layout without horizontal overflow for an image-classification demo; 16-class distribution grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flexbox with flex-wrap:wrap and flex:1 1 <basis>; set min-inline-size:0 on flex items with long unbreakable content so they shrink instead of overflowing; use repeat(auto-fit/auto-fill, minmax()) for card/result grids; logical properties + intrinsic sizing over fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the run panel is a .field-row flex container with two flex:1 1 280px columns each min-inline-size:0; the See-inside distribution is a repeat(auto-fit, minmax(180px,1fr)) grid and the practical queues a repeat(auto-fill, minmax(220px,1fr)) grid; long labels use overflow-wrap:anywhere. Mobile 360px showed zero horizontal overflow on all five pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headless overflow check false on main/basics/practical/wild/multimodel; ladder mobile screenshots retained under /home/paulkinlan/tmp

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low, move heavy inference off the main thread into a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy synchronous work off the main thread; yield/paint between chunks so the browser stays responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all model load + inference run in a module Web Worker (worker.js); the practical batch loop awaits one worker result per page (yielding between images) rather than a single blocking loop; the wild page debounces + single-flights re-classification.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: classify ~524 ms in the worker, page interactive during load, 0 console errors across all pages

guidanceConsulted.query: announce loading/inference errors and status accessibly in a live region
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Surface programmatic state at the same moment as the visual state; announce errors/status via aria-live regions rather than console.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — loader status and per-run #runStatus are role=status aria-live=polite; failures set a visible .err message on the page (never console-only) and the shared loader exposes a Retry path.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region + errors-on-the-page derived; run status text captured headless (Classifying… → Done.)

guidanceConsulted.query: cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔model navigation
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use @view-transition { navigation: auto } in the shared stylesheet so catalogue↔page navigation animates.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inherited from the shared public/styles.css (@view-transition navigation:auto); the DiT pages link it and use the shared design system (light+dark, WCAG AA), so navigation transitions and theming match the rest of the showcase.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; light + dark screenshots both legible

openQuestions: The synthetic samples are self-created (CC0) and classify sensibly, but real scanned RVL-CDIP pages carry noise/skew the model was trained on — would a small set of rights-safe REAL scans (e.g. public-domain government forms) sharpen the demo without a licensing gap?
Would a WebGPU execution-provider path (with the honest WASM fallback retained) meaningfully speed up the BEiT backbone on capable devices, or is q8-on-WASM already fast enough at ~0.5 s/page?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a 'DiT vs generic ImageNet ViT on the same page' side-by-side so the layout-vs-object-recognition distinction (why a document classifier beats a photo classifier here) is legible.
Add a live-camera 'point at a document' wild demo that classifies getUserMedia frames with an honest no-camera/denied fallback (user-initiated per lib/capture-ux.js).
Source and bundle a small rights-safe set of REAL scanned documents (public-domain forms/letters/memos) alongside the synthetic samples, recorded in media/manifest.json.

summary: DiT (Xenova/dit-base-finetuned-rvlcdip) built as a DISTINCT document-AI image-classification demo: a BEiT ViT pre-trained on 42M document images and fine-tuned on RVL-CDIP that sorts a scanned page into 16 types by reading layout, before any OCR. Real off-main-thread transformers.js@3.7.5 inference (WASM q8, ~88 MB, shared auto-init loader); full 16-class distribution + entropy/margin See-inside; four runnable use-case rungs (basics, a mailroom auto-sorter with confidence gating, an ambiguous-splice wild demo, and a classify→route→extract multi-model chain with Donut that answered '$588.60' on the invoice). Matrix-verified mobile+desktop, light+dark, console-clean, zero horizontal overflow. Runnability pre-verified (real 16-class head, not head-less).
